title: Local density of states distribution and multifractal eigenvectors of weighted random networks via the cavity approach
abstract: We study the local density of states (LDoS) distribution of a general class of weighted Erd≈ës-R√©nyi graphs. Using the cavity method, we obtain a good approximation to the full LDoS distribution and compact expressions for its power-law tails, which we show to have exponent $3$ in the extended phase. We deduce that the eigenvectors in the continuous part of the spectrum are extended but (weakly) multifractal, and we extract expressions for the associated fractal dimensions and the singularity spectrum. We also demonstrate that the inverse participation ratio in this multifractal phase exhibits an unusual logarithmic scaling with system size, which is neither fully-extended nor localised by the usual definitions. Finally, we verify that some symmetry properties (derived from the non-linear sigma model), which have been shown to hold for many systems exhibiting multifractality, also hold in our case, both for the LDoS distribution and the singularity spectrum.
summary: This theoretical paper analyzes the spectral properties of weighted random networks by studying the Local Density of States (LDoS) distribution. Using the cavity method, it determines that the LDoS exhibits a universal power-law tail with an exponent of 3. Furthermore, it characterizes the corresponding eigenvectors as weakly multifractal, providing quantitative measures like fractal dimensions and scaling exponents.
main_problem: The study investigates the local density of states (LDoS) distribution and the multifractal nature of eigenvectors in weighted random networks.
main_result: The LDoS distribution is found to exhibit a universal power-law tail with an exponent of 3, indicating that the corresponding eigenvectors are weakly multifractal. The system shows unusual logarithmic scaling for the Inverse Participation Ratio (IPR).
method: The analysis heavily relies on the cavity method and advanced techniques from Random Matrix Theory, including perturbative expansions and saddle-point approximations.
model_or_system: The model is a weighted Erd≈ës-R√©nyi graph ensemble characterized by weighted adjacency matrices $J$. The investigation analyzes spectral properties across different phases (extended, localized, multifractal).
key_observables: Local Density of States ($ho_i(\omega)$), Singularity Spectrum ($f(\alpha)$), Fractal Dimensions ($D(q)$), and the Inverse Participation Ratio ($I_2$).
important_parameters: The analysis is sensitive to the weight distribution, particularly requiring a non-zero probability mass at zero for certain exponents. The system behavior is analyzed in the thermodynamic limit ($N 	o \infty$).
main_assumptions: Key approximations include truncating moment expansions (e.g., $O(p^{-2})$) and using Laplace's method to derive asymptotic forms for the LDoS distribution.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the global density of states, the LDoS power-law tail behavior, and the singularity spectrum derived from eigenvector statistics.
paper_structure: The paper systematically develops approximations for the resolvent elements using the cavity method to determine the LDoS distribution. It then relates the asymptotic tails of this distribution to the scaling properties of the eigenvectors, deriving fractal dimensions and symmetry relations.
why_interesting: The findings on weak multifractality and non-trivial IPR scaling provide quantitative benchmarks for understanding spectral localization transitions in complex disordered quantum systems, which is relevant to many open quantum system models.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
